key_insight: Foundation ML interatomic potentials can capture simple geometric order in correlated oxides, but they still struggle with symmetry-sensitive bond/orbital order and fail on purely local spin-state transitions.
engineering_relevance: For materials modeling workflows, this means MLIPs may be reliable for some structural dynamics in complex oxides, but DFT or specialized models remain necessary when orbital and spin ordering control the physics.
method: Tested three foundation MLIPs with no system-specific training
Ran 1 ns molecular dynamics on 80- and 160-atom supercells
Evaluated low-temperature order in LaMnO3, LaCoO3, and NdNiO3 from 50 to 300 K
evidence_signal: The models were benchmarked on three distinct correlated perovskites spanning scalar, vector, and on-site order, revealing a clear hierarchy of success: NdNiO3 was captured, LaMnO3 only partly captured, and LaCoO3 remained inaccessible.
watch_item: Performance may look strong on average while still missing the correct symmetry or local electronic state, so order parameters must be checked explicitly rather than relying on structural fidelity alone.
paper_title: Bond, orbital and spin order in d4/d6/d7 perovskite oxides: successes and limitations of foundation interatomic potentials
